meaning: n.<俚>无期徒刑犯,职业军人
examples: Rick Wagoner, a GM lifer, had just taken on the top job.
Jeremiah Ellis is not an Army lifer. He has other plans.
But he is also a BOE lifer who has been a principal architect of the new regulatory agenda.
His surprise successor, a little-known Merck lifer named Richard Clark, was given carte blanche to turn the company around.
